trails.name: Taua tapu track
trails.description: This rural track between Airlie Road and Plimmerton features extensive views out to Mana Island, Porirua harbours and the surrounding countryside, and on a fine calm day is an ideal walk with a moderate challenge. The track is grass, natural surface.

trails.name: Camborne walkway
trails.description: This is a sheltered, flat walkway on the shores of the Pauatahanui Inlet, with spectacular views across the inlet to Whitby. It is home to the colourful, iconic Camborne Boatsheds. A gravel track at 2.2km in length, takes approximately 45 minutes to walk

trails.name: Southern clifftop walkway
trails.description: This is a 2.5km walk over hilltops with views over Titahi Bay and out to Mana Island. It will take around 1 hour return. Parts of the track are sealed, others are grass or gravel. Bikers can ride the sealed parts of the route from Moki Street into Stuart

trails.name: Spicer botanical park
trails.description: The Spicer Botanical Park is an arboretum of exotic trees which is directly beside the Department of Conservation (DoC) Colonial Knob Scenic Reserve. It is one of Porirua City's hidden gems, with over 2km of grass or gravel tracks in a series of short lo

trails.name: Titahi bay beach pathway
trails.description: Good surfing beach and occasionally during the year at low tide, remnants of sub fossil forest can be visible at the ends of Titahi Bay Beach.

trails.name: Ivey bay reserve walkway
trails.description: This 200m walkway zigzags to Kiriwai Road through manuka forest, or follow the 700m track up through regenerating native forest to Kahu Road for views of the entrance to Porirua Harbour.

trails.name: Carvel walkway
trails.description: Walkway through regenerating manuka forest that links Postgate Drive with Carvel Land and Kahu Road.

trails.name: Brandon reserve walkway
trails.description: A 610m path linking the Quarterdeck, Meridian Way, The Topdeck and lookout. There are views from north-west over Pauatahanui Estuary and north-east over Transmission Gully.

trails.name: Whitby upper lake pathway
trails.description: This is a 1.7 kilometre walk around manicured lower lake and then across Discovery Drive to the natural bushclad upper lake. Takes 20 minutes one way. Grade is easy, but wheelchair access is restricted.

trails.name: Stemhead walkway
trails.description: This is a 750m loop that connects Stemhead Reserve Playground with Cleat street, Staysail Place and Omapere Street.

trails.name: Conclusion walkway
trails.description: Starting at Whitford Brown Avenue, traversing along the ridgeline around Doncaster Terrace, returning to Whitford Brown Avenue via Ascot Park. There are excellent views from Pauatahanui Estuary in the north, to Waitangirua and Cannons Creek in the south

trails.name: Papakowhai walkway
trails.description: This is a 1.6 kilometre linking network of short, easy walkways through large tracts of manuka and native bush. The tracks are a mix of concrete paving, wooden boardwalk and bush tracks.

trails.name: Papakowhai reserve walkway
trails.description: A meandering path through Papakowhai Reserve with a mix of bushwalks and boardwalks.

trails.name: Samwell reserve walkway
trails.description: The largest native tree in Porirua is a giant Kaihikatea, over 300 years old. It is 5 minutes from the corner of Joseph Banks Drive and Samwell Drive.

trails.name: Navigation reserve walkway
trails.description: A kilometre long path with multiple entrypoints, providing easy access to Navigation Drive, Samwell Drive and Joseph Banks Drive.

trails.name: Adventure park pathway
trails.description: Links Furneaux Grove, Latitude Close and Discovery Drive through Adventure Park

trails.name: Hicks walkway
trails.description: Concrete path through Manuka Forest

trails.name: Spinnaker reserve walkway
trails.description: A series of connecting paths where at the top there are panoramic views spanning almost all of Whitby, Mana and Pauatahanui Inlet.

trails.name: Onepoto park pathway
trails.description: A 700m track through a recently planted hillside next to Onepoto Park with views over Pauatahanui Inlet

trails.name: The mainsail walkway
trails.description: 550m walkway from the Mainsail to Adventure Drive

trails.name: Golden gate
trails.description: Low tide 2.3km walk around the Peninsula offering inlet views. Rugged footwear is needed as it is across tidal rocks and sand.

trails.name: BLACK JACK TRACK
trails.description: A loop track in Pukatea/Whites Bay with views of Cook Strait.

trails.name: WHAKANUI TRACK
trails.description: This track leads from Wainuiomata to the Ōrongorongo River and offers views of the Remutaka Range and surrounding areas.

trails.name: MCKERROW TRACK
trails.description: Get panoramic views of Wainuiomata and the Hutt Valley as you climb to the top of Mt McKerrow.

trails.name: WAIRAU LAGOONS WALKWAY
trails.description: This flat loop track, ideal for kids, follows the lagoon shoreline with good bird watching to the rusting remains of the Waverley ship.

trails.name: BIG BEND TRACK
trails.description: The Big Bend Track follows the Ōrongorongo River and connects the Ōrongorongo Track with the Whakanui Track.

trails.name: CLAY RIDGE TRACK
trails.description: Climb up to Mt McKerrow for a great view of Wellington harbour and the surrounding hills near the top.

trails.name: Gun Emplacements to Makara Road End
BUTCHER TRACK
trails.description: Butcher Track provides magnificent views of the Wellington harbour and surrounding areas.

trails.name: QCT Endeavour Inlet to Resolution Bay

PUKAKA VALLEY TRACK
trails.description: This forested valley is part of Mt Robertson Scenic Reserve and provides good tramping and hunting opportunities and gives alternative access to the Mount Robertson loop and summit tracks.

trails.name: Motuara Island Lookout Track
trails.description: Experience stunning views over Queen Charlotte Sound and spot some native birds on this walk up to the island's summit.

trails.name: Cattle Ridge Track
trails.description: This track gives outstanding views of the Remutaka Range and Ōrongorongo Valley.

trails.name: Five Mile Loop Track
trails.description: This easy track passes through superb groves of mature hard beech in the lower reaches of Graces Stream.

trails.name: Mākara Walkway
trails.description: This loop walk climbs to vantage points with spectacular views – farmland, a beach and gun emplacements will keep kids interested.

trails.name: Matiu/Somes Island Loop Track
trails.description: A loop track around Matiu/Somes Island offers 360 degree views of Wellington harbour - with native wildlife, remnants from the world wars, historic quarantine buildings, and a lighthouse, there is plenty to keep kids interested.

trails.name: Mount Stokes Track
trails.description: This tramping track in Kenepuru Sound follows a ridge to the summit (1203 m), the highest point in the Sounds.

trails.name: Mt Matthews
trails.description: Fit trampers can climb the tallest peak in the Remutaka range (941m) and the nearest to Wellington.

trails.name: Mt McKerrow
trails.description: Get panoramic views of Wainuiomata and the Hutt Valley as you climb to the top of Mt McKerrow.

trails.name: Ōrongorongo Track
trails.description: This popular, easy walk passes through different forest types and has swimming holes at the end of the track - it's ideal for families.

trails.name: Rangituhi/Colonial Knob Walk
trails.description: Climb to the summit of this 468 m peak for views on clear day from the Inland Kaikoura Range in the south to Mt Taranaki in the north-west - it's a good option for older children. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Whites Bay tracks
trails.description: Whites Bay has walks for a range of abilities, including kids - there are rocky areas for scrambling and exploring and a near-flat sandy beach that's excellent for swimming too.

trails.name: Mount Robertson tracks
trails.description: Mountain bike or walk through mature and regenerating forest, with the option to visit the Mt Robertson/Tokomaru summit.

trails.name: Oruawairua Walk
trails.description: Experience the stunning views, native wildlife and history on Blumine Island (Oruawairua).

trails.description: This is a short walk in inner Queen Charlotte Sound.

trails.name: Boulder Hill Ride +Horse Riding
trails.description: The track just above the main Dry Creek car park will take you up to Buchanans Road. Follow this up to a plateau, then continue along the marked track to Boulder Hill. Return the same way.

trails.name: Colonial Knob Walkway
trails.description: The track passes through private farmland in places, keep to the marked route. Weather on hill tops can quickly change so make sure you're equipped with warm clothes, food and water and good walking shoes.

trails.name: Makara Walkway
trails.description: Carry food and drink. There is no drinkable water on the track.

trails.name: Waitaria Bay Walkway
trails.description: The Waitaria Bay Walkway entrance is located on Kenepuru Road and the poled route leads walkers across private land to the 4km-long reserve overlooking Kenepuru Sound.

trails.name: City to Sea Walkway
trails.description:
The City to Sea Walkway starts in the heart of central Wellington near Parliament and ends in Island Bay. The trail takes you up and down Wellington’s ridges, connecting parks and peaks, and ends at the south coast in Island Bay. The walk is no simple stroll, taking 6 - 7 hours, but can be done in stages. If you want to start in Island Bay, you can flip the route and walk sea to city.

Along the way, you will:

Pass several observatories, see the famous Cable Car, Victoria University and a plant nursery
Walk through Aro Valley with its turn-of-the-century cottages
Cross 14 parks and reserves, several children’s play areas and a public golf course
Walk along bushy Town Belt tracks, exposed ridgetops and through native and exotic vegetation
Get spectacular views of central Wellington, the southern suburbs of Newtown, Berhampore and Island Bay, Government House, historic Erskine College, the Brooklyn wind turbine, Mount Victoria, Mount Kaukau, and Cook Strait
See the more distant views of the Tararua, Remutaka and Kaikoura ranges

trails.name: Eastern Walkway
trails.description:
The Eastern Walkway is a point to point trail dedicated to walkers/runners that runs above the southern end of Miramar Peninsula.

Walk through spectacular native bush both up and down along the ridgeline and enjoy sweeping views of the coast. Pause and reflect at the Ataturk Memorial.

You can complete the walkway in either direction either from Tarakena Bay to Pass of Branda or the other way round.

If you’re starting at Tarakena Bay on Wellington’s south coast head uphill to the Ataturk Memorial. The walk continues in the open until you pass Bury Grove and head into the bush. After up and downhill you will come to Sidlaw Street junction. From here you walk along the ridge. You come to a junction where you can go left and out to Signallers Grove or right to Pass of Branda. Take the right downhill to Pass of Branda and you will soon be out in the open again. You drop down reasonably steeply to the road at the Pass of Branda.

trails.name: Johnston Hill Loop
trails.description:
Johnston Hill is one of the most prominent peaks in the Outer Green Belt.

The forest in this area is dominated by tawa and kohekohe, with a number of old podocarps still above the canopy. The canopy is quite diverse with rewarewa, hinau and a number of other species.

trails.name: Khandallah Park Loop Track / Mount Kaukau Summit
trails.description:
Mount Kaukau is a Wellington favourite for locals and visitors alike. It offers classic views of Wellington hilltops and harbour, and the South Island on a clear day. Climbing up hills and steps through native bush, you will make your way to the peak on open farmland and then travel down from there. 

Directions to complete the loop
Starting at Simla Crescent the walk goes along the Northern Walkway up to the top of Mount Kaukau where there are picnic areas. At the summit, you go onto the Skyline Walkway and come back down behind Khandallah Swimming Pool.

Start at Simla Crescent Khandallah. Follow the Northern Walkway from the entrance to Khandallah Park to the summit of Mt Kaukau. Continue all the way to the top. There are picnic areas and seats on the way up.

At the summit, the Northern Walkway joins the Skyline Walkway. You will now be walking on a 4WD track towards Sirsi Terrace. Stay on the 4WD track until you come to a sign pointing to the swimming pool.

Head back into the pines down a flight of steps. This track will take you back to the parking area behind the Khandallah Swimming Pool in Woodmancote Road.

Walk past the playground in the park and take the walkway to Clark Street. From the car park in Clark Street follow the signs back into the bush and returning to Simla Crescent.

Directions to Mt Kaukau Summit direct
The most direct route to Mt Kaukau Summit is to start at Simla Crescent entrance and take the first main trail on the left as marked. The track follows the main ridge to the summit. Allow around 35 minutes to get to the summit or an hour return.

trails.name: Northern Walkway
trails.description:
The Northern Walkway is a half-day journey along Wellington’s outer Green Belt, from Johnsonville to the Botanic Gardens via Te Ahumairangi Hill (Tinakori Hill), Trelissick Park and Mount Kaukau.

You’ll wind through hilly bush-clad terrain and discover abandoned railway tunnels and the city’s oldest scenic reserve. From the highest vantage points along the trail you’ll be treated to 360-degree views from the Remutaka Ranges and back towards the city and the sea.

If you’re short on time, the trail can be completed in stages.

trails.name: Otari Wilton's Bush
trails.description:
Choose from a variety of walks in New Zealand’s only botanic garden dedicated solely to native plants. See thriving natural New Zealand plant and wildlife, with native birds around each bend.

Check out the plant collections, grouped by the ecosystems found in different New Zealand regions, or head deeper into the bush to stand amongst green giants in the city’s only remaining original broadleaf-podocarp forest.

trails.name: Sanctuary to Sea Walkway
trails.description:
The walkway begins at sea level in Trelissick Park, near the bottom of Ngaio Gorge Road, and it finishes at the Zealandia wildlife sanctuary in Karori. 

The walkway follows Kaiwharawhara Stream past historic gunpowder magazines and through lush, regenerating bush for the length of Trelissick Park.

The walkway then continues along Waikowhai Street to Otari-Wilton's Bush, where it follows Kaiwharawhara Stream to an old part of Karori Cemetery. It then passes the Seaforth Terrace side of Ian Galloway Park to Paisley Terrace.

After crossing the main road, the walkway skirts around Appleton Park to Zealandia.

For public transport options, check the Metlink website.

trails.name: Southern Walkway
trails.description:
The Southern Walkway starts in Wellington's CBD and follows the Town Belt through some of the city's best loved parks and suburbs, all the way to Island Bay on the rugged South Coast.

Take in the sights of Oriental Bay then climb to the top of Mount Victoria to enjoy spectacular views from the summit lookout. You'll also see a famous location from The Lord of the Rings. Then descend through Sinclair Park to The Esplanade, and continue along the coastline of Taputeranga Marine Reserve to finish in Island Bay. Make the return trip or start from the opposite direction, or you can even complete the trail in stages.

The northern section of this trail is for walkers only, but there are lots of mountain biking routes on Mount Victoria. The southern section is open to both walker and bikers.

trails.name: Wellington Botanic Gardens
trails.description:
Stroll through Wellington Botanic Garden and enjoy the range of collections. Combine these walks to see all the sights, walking from 10 min - 2 hours. Bring a lunch and make it an outing, or escape from the office for a quick break. 
Downhill Path - Venture from the Cable Car downhill and stop to enjoy the main collections in the Botanic Garden- from the Australian to the Succulent Garden.

Kowhai Walk - Explore the top of the Botanic Garden and walk between the Dominion Observatory, Carter Observatory, Krupp Gun and Space Place.

Sculpture Trail - Start at the Lady Norwood Rose Garden and loop around the Sculpture Trail to spot 6 works amongst the flora (30 - 45 minutes).

Bush Walk – Walk through native New Zealand bush from the Cable Car downhill or reverse the trail to end with the harbour vista. Spot the wide range of native birds who are common sights in the Botanic Garden skies.

Sensation Trail - Louis Braille Trail – Pick up a Braille brochure from the Treehouse Visitor Centre to venture through the gardens to find tactile markers on the ground that indicate Braille sign posts nearby.

trails.name: Percy's Reserve   Waterfall Track
trails.description:
Percy’s Scenic Reserve is located on the lower slopes of Maungaraki and Korokoro. The area commonly
described as ‘Percy’s’ contains a lowland forest gully, a small cave containing weta and glowworm, a waterfall,
propagation nursery, gardens, and significant rare and endangered plant collections. Percy’s is part of a
connected network of reserves in Korokoro and Maungaraki. The reserve contains a number of historic features
like the water wheel.

trails.name: Manawa Karioi   Moemoeā
trails.description:
In the process of making new signs, we have been given new track names from the whānau at Tapu te Ranga Marae. The Tapu te Ranga Trust would like Manawa Karioi to be used and enjoyed by everyone.

Moemoeā - To have a dream, to have a vision:

From this highest track you can look out across the land and see the fruition of dreams past or dream new dreams of your own.

trails.name: Manawa Karioi   Te Ahi Kā   Loop Track
trails.description:
In the process of making new signs, we have been given new track names from the whānau at Tapu te Ranga Marae. The Tapu te Ranga Trust would like Manawa Karioi to be used and enjoyed by everyone. 

Te Ahi Kā - The long burning fires

This loop track encompasses most of Manawa Karioi. Its name refers to the home fires that symbolise a group’s title to land through continuous land occupation.

trails.name: Manawa Karioi   Whakatūranga
trails.description:
In the process of making new signs, we have been given new track names from the whānau at Tapu te Ranga Marae. The Tapu te Ranga Trust would like Manawa Karioi to be used and enjoyed by everyone. 

Whakatūranga - Establishment or formation

This track runs through the heart of Manawa Karioi, including Seed Source Gully. It is named for the core values of the Tapu te Ranga Trust, which are whanaungatanga, kaitiakitanga, turangawaewae and rangatiratanga.
